title: poinsettia morisoniana
intro: Poinsettia morisoniana, also known as the Fire Poinsettia, is a species of flowering plant in the Euphorbiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of southern China. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its latex and leaves, which can be used to treat wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant is also known for its health benefits, which include reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and providing antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the leaves and latex of Poinsettia morisoniana are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be applied topically or taken orally.
